title: epipactis phyllanthes subsp. confusa
intro: Epipactis phyllanthes subsp. confusa, also known as the Confused Helleborine, is a subspecies of orchid native to Europe. The plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Epipactis phyllanthes subsp. confusa include its ability to aid in wound healing and reduce fever, as well as its potential to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a tea, tincture, or infused in oil to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
